Shishir Agrawal
  • Mathematics
  • Teaching
  • Miscellaneous

Overview

I study arithmetic geometry: more specifically, I am interested in p-adic cohomology theories and p-adic analytic geometry.

Here are some mathematical phrases that I find exciting/intriguing: p-adic, nonarchimedean, adic space, perfectoid, pro-étale, overconvergent, (bounded) Robba ring, (arithmetic) D-module, rigid cohomology, de Rham(-Witt), crystalline, cotangent complex, (higher) category, derived, homological, topos, derivator, non-noetherian, ...

I completed my PhD at UC Berkeley in 2018. My advisor was David Nadler.

Papers

All papers at arXiv.org/a/agrawal_s_1

  • Deformations of overconvergent isocrystals on the projective line. arXiv:1710.03404. Submitted.

My PhD thesis is available here. There are several errata in the document. Most are not terribly serious, but I suggest that readers look at the arXiv version. However, it's worth noting that the arXiv version does omit some of the expository content of the thesis.

Miscellaneous

Here are some slides for presentations I've given.

  • A Smörgåsbord of Arithmetic Geometry. Slides for a talk I gave at Colorado College's Fearless Fridays seminar in February 2018.

Here are some miscellaneous mathematical writings.

  • Off on a tangent. Some notes about derivatives.
  • Exponential at infinity. A fun explicit calculation I did during summer 2017 involving an irregular rank 1 overconvergent differential module over the Robba ring.
  • Contangent complex. Some incomplete notes I compiled when I was preparing for a talk I gave in David Nadler's Fall 2013 Geometric Representation Theory Seminar.
  • Fundamental group of a curve. An expository term paper from Martin Olsson's Spring 2012 Algebraic Geometry course.

If you find errors in any of these, please let me know!
